summary: Verified the new patient-facing AI visit-brief surface (GET/POST /api/portal/visit-brief + PortalVisitBriefCard). Backend pytest /app/backend/tests/test_portal_visit_brief.py = 5/5 pass (auth: admin 403 + unauth 401/403; happy path: 200 + full brief shape + cached:true on 2nd call; regenerate: cached:false; PHI hygiene: no ICD codes). Browser-side fetch from authenticated patient session also verified end-to-end: 1st call cached:true (warm cache from prior test), keys = [headline, last_visit, your_progress, this_visit, ask_about, reminders], 3 reminders, 2 ask_about, headline = 'Welcome back, Ethan — your progress is looking great!' (patient-friendly second-person tone, no jargon). 2nd call cached:true. POST regenerate cached:false. UI render check on /portal was logged-and-skipped per the review_request: PortalOverview.jsx mounts <PortalVisitBriefCard /> only when upcoming_appointments.length > 0, but /api/portal/overview returns 403 'Portal session not bound to a patient' for the password-login session (this is a pre-existing portal-binding constraint, not introduced by this iteration), so upcoming_appointments is empty and the card is correctly hidden. Confirmed the conditional mount logic is correct by direct cookie-fetch of the visit-brief endpoint inside the same browser session — endpoint serves Ethan's brief perfectly. Light portal regression: Upcoming appointments / Questionnaires / Booking requests cards still render unchanged.

integration_issues.issue: /api/portal/overview returns 403 'Portal session not bound to a patient' for password-login sessions (only SMS-OTP-bound portal sessions are bound to a patient). Side effect for THIS iteration: PortalVisitBriefCard never mounts via password login because upcoming_appointments stays empty, even though /api/portal/visit-brief itself works perfectly with the same cookie. Pre-existing constraint, not a regression. Worth noting since it limits how the new card can be QA'd through the password login path.

action_items: Optional: relax the PortalOverview mount condition (or fall back to fetching /api/portal/visit-brief directly) so the card renders for any authenticated patient with a usable brief, even when /api/portal/overview is gated by SMS-OTP binding. Today the card is invisible for any patient who logs in via password instead of the SMS-OTP /portal flow. Backend brief endpoint already accepts the password session — this is purely a frontend gating issue.
Optional: surface a friendlier UX when /api/portal/overview returns 'Portal session not bound to a patient' (currently the page silently shows 'No upcoming appointments.' which is misleading).
critical_code_review_comments: PortalVisitBriefCard.jsx: clean, well-scoped — has data-testids on every meaningful node, soft-fails to null on fetch errors, and respects the optional fields with isNonEmpty guards. No issues.

Mount gate (upcoming_appointments.length > 0) means a patient with NO upcoming visit will never see the brief — that's the documented intent, but combined with the SMS-OTP-binding restriction on /api/portal/overview it makes the card unreachable via password login. Consider decoupling: render the card whenever the visit-brief endpoint returns a non-empty brief, regardless of the upcoming list.
test_portal_visit_brief.py covers: auth (admin/unauth), happy path + cache hit, regenerate, and PHI hygiene (ICD regex). Good baseline regression file.

success_rate.frontend: Verified what was testable: API integration via browser cookie 100%; explicit UI card render skipped per spec because Ethan has no upcoming appointment in the password-login portal view. Regression cards 100%.

context_for_next_testing_agent: All AI smart-cache and patient visit-brief endpoints + their tests are green. The only thing curl/browser-fetch couldn't validate end-to-end is the on-screen render of <PortalVisitBriefCard /> because Ethan Parker currently has no upcoming appointments in the portal-overview endpoint (and overview is also blocked by SMS-OTP binding for password sessions). If next iteration wants to UI-test the card, options: (a) seed an upcoming appointment for patient_id=ccms-voice-notes and bypass the portal-binding gate, or (b) loosen the PortalOverview mount condition so the card renders directly when the brief endpoint returns content (recommended in action_items). Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_portal_visit_brief.py is the canonical regression — keep it.
rca of the issue: No bug to RCA. The 'card not visible on /portal' observation is by design: PortalOverview mounts the card conditionally on upcoming_appointments.length > 0, and the test patient has none in the current overview response (compounded by /api/portal/overview returning 403 'Portal session not bound to a patient' for password-login sessions — which is a pre-existing binding rule, not part of this iteration). The visit-brief endpoint itself is fully functional for the password session, as confirmed both by pytest and by an in-browser fetch.
